task_updates.executor_notes: Audited `useTimelineSave.ts` and documented the three concern clusters, their dependency graph, all shared refs crossing the cluster boundaries, and proposed acyclic interfaces for `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync` in `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/restructure-the-video-editor-20260406-0206/t1-useTimelineSave-audit.md`. Verified the audit explicitly captures the commit<->persistence bridge, the poll/sync dependencies on save refs, and the shared-ref contract needed for the next extraction step.

Extracted `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync`, then rewired `useTimelineSave` to compose the shared refs and preserve the downstream interface. Verification: `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` passed with 21 files / 62 tests, and `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit` passed. Added `usePollSync.test.ts` to cover the `editSeq/savedSeq/isSaving/pendingOps` matrix: all non-saving cases match the legacy accept/reject behavior, and the only deliberate new rejection is the approved `isSaving=true && savedSeq>=editSeq && pendingOpsRef===0` default-reject gate.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. The audit identifies the full shared-ref surface across commit/edit, save/persist, and poll/sync (`dataRef`, `editSeqRef`, `savedSeqRef`, `pendingOpsRef`, `configVersionRef`, `lastSavedSignature`, `selectedClipIdRef`, `selectedTrackIdRef`, `isSavingRef`) and proposes a `scheduleSaveRef` bridge so the extracted modules can be composed without circular dependencies.

Verified with the full video-editor Vitest module suite and `npx tsc --noEmit`. `usePollSync.test.ts` exercises the full `editSeq/savedSeq/isSaving/pendingOps` matrix: when `isSaving=false`, the extracted gate matches the previous blacklist decisions; when `isSaving=true` and the other idle conditions are met, polls are intentionally rejected as `saving`, which is the approved default-reject tightening for this phase.

Confirmed. `useTimelineSave.ts` is 88 lines, composes `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync`, and still exports `SaveStatus`, `shouldAcceptPolledData`, and the same callable `useTimelineSave` surface used by `useTimelineData`. The full video-editor suite and `tsc --noEmit` both passed, so no downstream consumer broke.

Confirmed. All video-editor callers now use `applyEdit`; `rg -n "applyTimelineEdit|applyResolvedConfigEdit" src/tools/video-editor` returns no remaining source usages. `createTrackAndMoveClip` still commits through the `type: 'config'` path in `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts`, so its existing no-overlap-resolution semantics are preserved, and the old wrappers were removed from the commit/data/context surfaces.

Confirmed. The shared types now live in `useTimelineData.types.ts`, the planned importer sites now reference that module instead of `useTimelineData.ts`, and the old `ReturnType<typeof useTimelineData>` alias was replaced with the explicit `UseTimelineDataResult` type. `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit` passed cleanly.

Confirmed. `useTimelineData.ts` is deleted, `useTimelineState.ts` now owns the three orchestration pieces that previously lived in the wrapper (`onSaveSuccessRef` wiring to `render.setRenderDirty`, `pruneSelectionRef`, and the `useLayoutEffect` bridge from `history.onBeforeCommit` to `save.onBeforeCommitRef`), and both `useDerivedTimeline` calls remain: one for `save.selectedClipId`/`save.selectedTrackId` and one for `primaryClipId` multi-select derivation. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed; the full video-editor Vitest module was also re-run and hit one unrelated existing failure in `ClipAction.test.tsx` outside this refactor path.

Confirmed. `resolveGroupOverlaps` now returns `metaPatches`, `applyMultiDragMoves` accumulates overlap patches across loop iterations with per-clip spread merge so existing `track` patches are preserved, and new direct tests were added for both `resolveOverlaps` and `resolveGroupOverlaps` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/__tests__/resolve-overlaps.test.ts`. The full video-editor Vitest module passed after the change.

Confirmed. `useDerivedTimeline` now derives `resolvedConfig` with `useMemo(() => data?.resolvedConfig ?? null, [data])`, and the targeted ESLint pass on the changed files completed cleanly, so the `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` rule is satisfied. The full video-editor suite and `tsc --noEmit` both passed after the simplification; no automated regression surfaced that required reverting the change.

Confirmed. `AudioTrack` now keys each `Sequence` with `clip.id`, `clip.at`, `clip.from ?? 0`, `clip.to ?? ''`, and `clip.speed ?? 1`, and the file includes a comment explaining that Remotion audio timing and playback-rate changes require remount during playback.

No. The video-editor suite passes (22 files / 70 tests) and `npx tsc --noEmit` succeeds, but the full app suite still fails in multiple unrelated shared/tooling areas outside the video-editor refactor. The structural checks confirm `useTimelineSave.ts` is a thin 87-line composer, `useTimelineData.ts` is deleted, the old edit-path symbols are gone, and the Editor/Chrome/Playback context split remains. The hook-hierarchy criterion is also not literally satisfied yet because `useTimelineState` still calls `useTimelineSave` rather than composing `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync` directly.
